dataset: Liverpool Cancer iChart (University of Liverpool drug interactions database)

pi_items.source: FDA documentation, PMC/NCBI medical literature confirming Vandetanib is an approved tyrosine kinase inhibitor for medullary thyroid cancer
All three confirmed as FDA-approved antidepressants: Citalopram (SSRI), Sertraline (SSRI), Mirtazapine (tetracyclic antidepressant) from Drugs.com and medical literature

FDA Drug Safety Communication (2011-2012) and comparative studies showing citalopram has dose-dependent QT prolongation risk (max 40mg/day for adults, 20mg/day for patients >60 years), while Sertraline and Mirtazapine have minimal or no QT prolongation risk. This makes Citalopram the highest-risk antidepressant among the three when combined with Vandetanib (which also causes QT prolongation)

Liverpool Cancer iChart application uses a traffic light system (red/orange for high-risk, yellow/green/grey for lower-risk interactions) as confirmed by multiple sources including cancer-druginteractions.org documentation and app store descriptions

Liverpool Cancer iChart traffic light system as documented in cancer-druginteractions.org: yellow indicates caution/moderate interaction, green indicates safe/minimal interaction, grey indicates insufficient evidence. This is the standard color scheme for the application
Confirmed in task.json description and export_result.sh script which explicitly references this Android device path for the output file

privileged_info_summary: Vandetanib is a real FDA-approved tyrosine kinase inhibitor for medullary thyroid cancer that causes dose-dependent QT prolongation (mean 30-35ms, with 69% of patients experiencing QTc >450ms). Citalopram is a real SSRI antidepressant with dose-dependent QT prolongation risk (FDA warning: max 40mg/day, 20mg/day for older patients). Sertraline and Mirtazapine are real antidepressants with minimal or no QT prolongation risk. The task uses the real Liverpool Cancer iChart application (developed by University of Liverpool and Radboud UMC) which employs a traffic light system (red/orange for high-risk, yellow/green/grey for safer interactions) to rate drug-drug interactions. Citalopram is appropriately identified as the highest-risk antidepressant among the three when combined with Vandetanib due to cumulative QT prolongation risk.
